beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check weather and work emails.
beats.reasoning: Mia wakes early as usual; the transit strike means she'll need to drive, so she's checking conditions and mentally planning around the closure. Mild weather makes for a pleasant morning routine.

beats.activity: Drive to Interbay office through light traffic (transit strike reduces congestion).
beats.reasoning: Metro closure actually helps Mia's commute—fewer buses mean clearer roads. She arrives at the office with time to spare before the day's showings.

beats.activity: Morning at office: fielding calls, emails, preparing two condo showings.
beats.reasoning: Steady morning of listing work; the Wallingford condo momentum from recent days carries forward. No showings until after lunch, giving her focused office time.

beats.activity: Lunch: grab fresh poke bowl at Uwajimaya food court, eat at desk.
beats.reasoning: Mia's an adventurous eater; Uwajimaya is a short detour from Interbay and feeds her curiosity. Mild 68-degree weather makes a quick errand feel easy, and she's back to the office by early afternoon.
Return commute is quick and uneventful; transit strike still means lighter traffic.

beats.activity: Two condo showings: one in Capitol Hill, one in Fremont.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon is dedicated to back-to-back showings in nearby neighborhoods. Mia stays mobile and focused on her core real estate work.

beats.activity: Drive to Ballard for food bank shift (transit strike ending soon, roads still clear).
beats.reasoning: Mia volunteers regularly on Thursdays; the transit strike doesn't affect her since she drives. Mild weather and good energy mean she's up for her shift.

beats.activity: Food bank volunteer shift: sorting donations, organizing shelves, talking with team.
beats.reasoning: This grounding work is core to Mia's identity. She values the community connection and purposeful labor; a Thursday evening shift fits her rhythm.

beats.activity: Drive home to Kirkland; stop at Whole Foods to pick up salmon and greens.
beats.reasoning: Quick errand on the way home; Mia likes cooking with fresh ingredients. The mild weather and 68-degree forecast make for a pleasant evening ahead.

beats.activity: Arrive home, put away groceries, prep kitchen.
beats.reasoning: Settling in after a full day; unpacking groceries and transitioning into evening mode.

beats.activity: Cook simple salmon dinner with roasted vegetables; eat while reading.; Write three handwritten letters; reflect on the day.
beats.reasoning: Mia unwinds with a meal she's prepared herself. Cooking grounds her after a full day of showings and volunteering.
Solid 7 hours 45 minutes of sleep; Mia is well-rested and ready for Friday. The day had good momentum and purposeful work.

diary:
Thursday turned out better than expected, even with the transit strike. The roads were practically empty driving out to Interbay this morning—silver lining to everyone staying home. Grabbed a poke bowl at Uwajimaya around lunch and ate it at my desk while reviewing the two showings I had lined up. Capitol Hill and Fremont, back-to-back. Neither one moved, but that's the job.

The real highlight was my shift at the food bank in Ballard. Something about sorting donations and organizing shelves just quiets my mind. My teammates were in good spirits despite the chaos outside. By the time I headed home, the strike was wrapping up and the roads were opening back up.

Cooked a simple salmon dinner and wrote three letters by hand—something I haven't done in months. There's something grounding about it. Felt less scattered by the end of the day than I have in weeks.
thought_process: The strike actually worked in my favor today, which felt oddly fortunate. No gridlock meant I could move between showings without stress, and that calm translated into better client interactions—even if nothing closed. I chose to grab lunch at Uwajimaya instead of my usual spot; sometimes a small change in routine refreshes perspective. The food bank shift was deliberate too. Work's been relentless lately, and I needed to be around people doing something tangible, not just chasing commission. Writing those letters at the end felt like a way to anchor myself back into what matters. Age 52 means I'm more intentional about how I spend evening hours now.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
